class.name: OclGodotMesher
class.brief_description: Batch meshing utility for OCCT graph handles.
class.description: Provides convenient batch meshing ([method mesh_faces], [method mesh_edges], [method mesh_vertices]) that converts OCCT topological entities into Godot mesh resources (ArrayMesh, MultiMesh, or PhysicsBody3D collision shapes). All meshing methods take an [OclGraphHandle] as the first argument and accept an optional [code]existing[/code] parameter that can be an ArrayMesh, MultiMesh, or PhysicsBody3D: - [b]ArrayMesh[/b] / [b]MultiMesh[/b]: surfaces/instances are cleared and re-created; user customizations (shader materials, etc.) are preserved. - [b]PhysicsBody3D[/b] (StaticBody3D, RigidBody3D, CharacterBody3D, ...): one CollisionShape3D child is added per feature, each with [code]occtl[/code] metadata containing a [code]feature_id[/code] key that maps back to the original face/edge/vertex node ID. Faces use ConcavePolygonShape3D, edges use CapsuleShape3D, and vertices use SphereShape3D.

method.name: mesh_faces
method.description: Triangulate faces into an [ArrayMesh] or create collision shapes on a [PhysicsBody3D]. - [code]graph[/code]: The graph handle (must be valid). - [code]existing[/code]: [code]null[/code], [ArrayMesh], or [PhysicsBody3D]. - [code]options[/code]: Meshing options controlling deflection, angle, etc. When [code]null[/code], defaults are used. - [code]face_ids[/code]: [code]null[/code] for all faces, or a [PackedInt64Array] of face node IDs. - [code]include_normals[/code]: Compute angle-thresholded smooth normals (sharp/smooth edges are handled automatically based on the angle limit in [code]options[/code]). - [code]include_uvs[/code]: Include UV coordinates if available. - [code]include_tangents[/code]: Compute tangent vectors (requires normals and UVs). - [code]include_feature_ids[/code]: Encode face node IDs as vertex colors (one colour per face).

method.name: mesh_edges
method.description: Build edge tube instances as a [MultiMesh] or create collision shapes on a [PhysicsBody3D]. A low-resolution cylinder mesh is instanced along each edge polyline segment using [MultiMesh] transforms — one cylinder per segment — for efficient rendering. - [code]graph[/code]: The graph handle (must be valid). - [code]existing[/code]: [code]null[/code], [MultiMesh], or [PhysicsBody3D]. - [code]options[/code]: Meshing options (controls cylinder resolution via the angle parameter). - [code]edge_ids[/code]: [code]null[/code] for all edges, or a [PackedInt64Array] of edge node IDs. - [code]radius[/code]: Tube radius. Default [code]0.01[/code]. If [code]<= 0[/code], uses [code]options.deflection * 10[/code]. The radius is automatically scaled by the graph's bounding box diagonal for consistent visual size across different models.

method.name: mesh_vertices
method.description: Build vertex sphere instances as a [MultiMesh] or create collision shapes on a [PhysicsBody3D]. A low-resolution sphere mesh is instanced at each vertex position using [MultiMesh] transforms for efficient rendering. - [code]graph[/code]: The graph handle (must be valid). - [code]existing[/code]: [code]null[/code], [MultiMesh], or [PhysicsBody3D]. - [code]options[/code]: Meshing options (controls sphere resolution via the angle parameter). - [code]vertex_ids[/code]: [code]null[/code] for all vertices, or a [PackedInt64Array] of vertex node IDs. - [code]radius[/code]: Sphere radius. Default [code]0.02[/code] (double the edge radius default). If [code]<= 0[/code], uses [code]options.deflection * 10[/code]. The radius is automatically scaled by the graph's bounding box diagonal.
